mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, contradicting the negative rate-return relationship.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market, defined as the unemployment rate rising by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, signals impending recessionary pressures that drive negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without monetary tightening pressure, generating positive forward equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of active periods, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a confirmed economic expansion reflects strengthening corporate output and capacity utilization, which translates into positive short-term S&P 500 momentum.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during expansion phases, or 5-day forward returns are negative in over 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates sustained labor market tightness and wage growth, supporting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll changes coincide with negative market returns.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in housing starts below 1.5 million units signals a severe downturn in the construction sector and broader consumer confidence, triggering negative forward equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in over 70% of instances where housing starts drop below the threshold, invalidating the bearish signal.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated consumer inflation and high policy rates creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and raises discount rates, forcing negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect yields positive forward returns in over 60% of triggered cases, or the joint threshold condition shows no statistical edge over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter GDP growth during the late stages of an economic cycle confirms resilient aggregate demand, which sustains corporate revenue growth and generates positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: GDP growth signals produce negative forward returns in over 55% of late-cycle observations, or the predictive accuracy drops below chance levels in walk-forward testing.
